channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Taiwan’s President Says He Trusts Trump to Approve Arms Sales
item.description: President Lai Ching-te made the hopeful remarks after Mr. Trump suggested that he could use a proposed $14 billion arms package for Taiwan as leverage with Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley

media:content.media:description: President Lai Ching-te of Taiwan at a news conference for foreign journalists in Taipei on Thursday.

item.title: Trump Signs Preliminary Deal to End the War With Iran
item.description: The White House confirmed that President Trump signed the memorandum of understanding between the United States and Iran on Wednesday at the Palace of Versailles.
item.dc:creator: Julie Yoon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Average U.S. Gasoline Price Falls Below $4 for First Time in Months
item.description: Drivers are enjoying some relief at the pump after the United States and Iran signed an agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Kailyn Rhone
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Gasoline prices rose sharply after the start of U.S.-led war with Iran.

item.title: Oil Prices Fall as U.S.-Iran Deal to Reopen Hormuz Takes Effect
item.description: Oil prices fell and stocks rose after Pakistan announced that the agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz would take effect immediately.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: El papa León visitará la aldea de la primera santa de EE. UU.
item.description: El pontífice llegará el sábado a una villa italiana donde nació la madre Cabrini, la primera santa estadounidense, y se espera que retome su enfoque incluyente sobre los migrantes.
item.dc:creator: Kathleen Sprows Cummings

media:content.media:credit: Naila Ruechel para The New York Times

item.title: When in Jersey for the World Cup, Do as the Locals Do. (Go to a Mall.)
item.description: There is no tailgating at MetLife Stadium, which is surrounded by swampland and highways. But there is a three-million-square-foot megamall nearby.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Keh

item.title: Together We Stand: A Knicks Celebration and a Pause for Communal Joy
item.description: The Knicks’ parade through the Canyon of Heroes on Thursday is expected to attract legions of fans celebrating the team’s first championship in 53 years.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich, Dana Rubinstein and Santul Nerkar

media:content.media:credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The crowded scene at a watch party in Greenwich Village on Saturday is expected to be replicated many times over at the Canyon of Heroes parade on Thursday.

item.title: Can a Nerd Still Get Elected to Congress in Manhattan?
item.description: In a Democratic primary for a coveted House seat in Manhattan, Micah Lasher is betting long experience can prevail despite anti-establishment headwinds.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos

media:content.media:credit: Anna Connors for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Assemblyman Micah Lasher, a Democrat running to replace Representative Jerrold Nadler in New York’s 12th Congressional District, has Mr. Nadler’s endorsement.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 18, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Feels ‘Hormuzled’ by Trump’s New Deal With Iran
item.description: Although the agreement finally reopens the Strait of Hormuz, “we threw in a minimum of $300 billion,” Kimmel said. “Right now, Melania’s wondering, ‘How do I get a deal like that?’”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Word of the Day: antipodes
item.description: This word has appeared in five articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Billionaire Tax Officially Has Enough Signatures for California Ballot
item.description: A proposed wealth tax on billionaires is headed to the ballot unless Gov. Gavin Newsom and other opponents reach a deal with backers to remove it.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla
item.category: Service Employees International Union-United Healthcare Workers West

media:content.media:credit: Mike Kai Chen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A proposed wealth tax on billionaires has qualified for the November ballot.

item.title: This Is Not a Grand Bargain With Iran. Good.
item.description: The world doesn’t need a nuclear deal with Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Michael Singh
item.category: Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by Sam Whitney/The New York Times

item.title: In Britain, the Far Right Is Setting the Agenda
item.description: Its opponents urgently need to demonstrate that they can do more than throw up their hands in horror.
item.dc:creator: William Davies

item.title: Elecciones en Inglaterra: esto hay que saber sobre las votaciones extraordinarias
item.description: El jueves hay elecciones extraordinarias en Makerfield, un distrito al norte de Inglaterra. Sus resultados podrían provocar la destitución del primer ministro Keir Starmer.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear

media:content.media:description: Andy Burnham, el alcalde del Gran Mánchester, dando un discurso en Wigan, Inglaterra, el sábado.

item.title: In Southeast Asia, Switching to Solar Offers Relief from Energy Crisis Caused by Iran War
item.description: Homeowners and businesses in the region, which relies heavily on imported oil, are installing rooftop solar panels to reduce the sting of soaring energy costs.
item.dc:creator: Zunaira Saieed and Aie Balagtas See
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Jes Aznar for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Setting up a solar power system at a home in Quezon City, Philippines, this month.

item.title: Why the By-Election in Makerfield Will Have Giant Consequences for the UK
item.description: Here is what to know about the by-election on Thursday in Makerfield, the outcome of which could lead to Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s ouster.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear

media:content.media:description: Andy Burnham, the mayor of Greater Manchester, giving a speech in Wigan, England, on Saturday.

item.title: Quote of the Day: NYNJ Stadium? A Cross-State Kick in the Teeth.
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, June 18, 2026.

item.title: Acuerdo entre EE. UU. e Irán: esto dice el texto
item.description: Periodistas de The New York Times ofrecen un análisis de los párrafos del acuerdo, que un alto cargo dio a conocer el miércoles.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament

item.title: Team Medic Recounts Iran’s ‘Stressful’ World Cup Debut in L.A.
item.description: A member of Iran’s medical team says the team endured delays, multiple security searches and a rushed exit, frustrating players and officials.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Iran’s Saeid Ezatolahi receiving medical attention during the match against New Zealand at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Calif., on Monday.

item.title: Highlights From the Las Culturistas Culture Awards Hosted by Bowen Yang and Matt Rogers
item.description: The comedians Bowen Yang and Matt Rogers honored the nooks and crannies of pop culture at the annual awards show.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Spencer

item.title: Knicks Accept a White House Invitation to Celebrate Title, Owner Says
item.description: James Dolan said he was “very proud” to bring his championship team to Washington. It would be the first N.B.A. team to visit the White House with Trump as president.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar and Tyler Pager

media:content.media:description: James Dolan, the owner of the Knicks, center, celebrated the team’s championship win on Saturday.

item.title: Kansas City Police Seek Gunman in Spree of Five Roadway Shootings
item.description: The attacks, which killed one and injured four, played out as the city was preparing to host its first World Cup match. An Uber driver taking Argentina fans to the stadium was among the victims.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 18, 2026
item.description: Scott Hogan gets long words out of short clues.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Bryan Anselm for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Piping hot puzzle here! Get your fresh Thursday puzzle, right out of the oven!

item.title: Before Making a Deal, Trump Demanded Iran’s Surrender. He Got a Surprise.
item.description: While the Iranians suffered substantial losses in the war, they emerged from a confrontation with the world’s most powerful military having proved they can use economic chaos as a weapon.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump at a Group of 7 summit in France on Wednesday. The president has argued he has the upper hand with an agreement that leaves negotiations over Iran’s nuclear program for a later date.

item.title: The Last 12 Weeks: Episode 5
item.description: One week before the execution, the defense team awaits a final ruling on David Wood’s life.
item.dc:creator: Maurice Chammah, Alvin Melathe, Jen Guerra, Anita Badejo, Adam Dorn aka Mocean Worker, Matthias Bossi and Jon Evans of Stellwagen Symphonette, Phoebe Wang and Catherine Anderson

item.title: Daveigh Chase, ‘Lilo & Stitch’ Voice Actor and ‘The Ring’ Villain, Dies at 35
item.description: She brought the plucky Lilo to life in the popular animated film series, and unnerved viewers as the child villain Samara in the horror film “The Ring.”
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald

media:content.media:description: Daveigh Chase in 2011. Her father said she had struggled with drugs since the age of 13.

item.title: The Last 12 Weeks: Episode 4
item.description: We visit death row, where David Wood tries to clear his name. But not before a victim of his crimes has her say.
item.dc:creator: Maurice Chammah, Alvin Melathe, Jen Guerra, Anita Badejo, Adam Dorn aka Mocean Worker, Matthias Bossi and Jon Evans of Stellwagen Symphonette, Phoebe Wang and Catherine Anderson

item.title: Trump’s Deal With Iran Opens New Rifts in G.O.P.
item.description: Some in the president’s party were skeptical about whether the agreement he reached included adequate concessions from Iranian officials.
item.dc:creator: Bayliss Wagner, Robert Jimison and Tim Balk
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: President Trump on Wednesday at the Group of 7 summit in Évian-les-Bains, France. Some Republican leaders have balked at the terms of Mr. Trump’s preliminary arrangement to end the war in Iran.

item.title: The Last 12 Weeks: Episode 3
item.description: In the final weeks before the execution date, the defense team has to decide just how far it’s willing to push to save David Wood’s life.
item.dc:creator: Maurice Chammah, Alvin Melathe, Jen Guerra, Anita Badejo, Adam Dorn aka Mocean Worker, Matthias Bossi and Jon Evans of Stellwagen Symphonette, Phoebe Wang and Catherine Anderson

item.title: The Last 12 Weeks: Episode 2
item.description: With less than two months until the execution, new witnesses offer unexpected revelations.
item.dc:creator: Maurice Chammah, Alvin Melathe, Jen Guerra, Anita Badejo, Adam Dorn aka Mocean Worker, Matthias Bossi and Jon Evans of Stellwagen Symphonette, Phoebe Wang and Catherine Anderson

item.title: The Last 12 Weeks: Episode 1
item.description: There are uphill battles … and then there’s David Wood’s case.
item.dc:creator: Maurice Chammah, Alvin Melathe, Jen Guerra, Anita Badejo, Adam Dorn aka Mocean Worker, Matthias Bossi and Jon Evans of Stellwagen Symphonette, Phoebe Wang and Catherine Anderson

item.title: Tornadoes Reported in Wisconsin and Illinois as Violent Storms Sweep the Midwest
item.description: The National Weather Service issued several of its highest-level tornado alerts on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Nazaneen Ghaffar and Amy Graff

item.title: Warsh Makes His Case With Jargon, and a Penchant for Detail
item.description: The Federal Reserve’s new chairman held a press conference where he bantered with reporters and laid out a vision for change at the central bank.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Ember

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kevin M. Warsh, the Federal Reserve chairman, after the central bank’s meeting in Washington on Wednesday.

item.title: Boyle Heights Warehouse Fire Prompts Thousands of L.A. Residents to Shelter in Place
item.description: Heavy black smoke was visible across much of Los Angeles as flames tore through a nearly 500,000-square-foot building just east of downtown.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín

media:content.media:description: Black smoke rising from a warehouse fire in the Boyle Heights neighborhood of Los Angeles on Wednesday.

item.title: Robert Thurman, Leading Expert on Tibetan Buddhism and Uma Thurman’s Father, Dies at 84
item.description: A former monk who was also Uma Thurman’s father, he made sure Buddhism retained its intellectual and spiritual rigor as it spread through the West.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

media:content.media:credit: Astrid Stawiarz/Getty Images for Tibet House
media:content.media:description: Robert Thurman in 2015. He brought an infectious energy to the endless lectures and conferences he organized around Buddhism.

item.title: Prosecutors Say Man Used Charity Scheme to Raise Thousands for Hamas
item.description: The suspect used social media accounts, crowd-funding sites and a charity to solicit hundreds of thousands of dollars in donations, a complaint said.
item.dc:creator: Christina Caron

media:content.media:credit: Jose A. Alvarado Jr. for The New York Times

item.title: Hot Mics at G7 Summit Offer Glimpse Into Leaders’ Conversations
item.description: Microphones left on during the Group of 7 summit in Évian-les-Bains, France, picked up side conversations between world leaders as they discussed Greenland and cigarettes.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:description: Kristalina Georgieva, the head of the International Monetary Fund, speaking with some leaders of G7 countries in Evian-les-Bains, France, on Wednesday.

item.title: How Does Trump’s Deal With Iran Compare to Obama’s?
item.description: The two agreements are difficult to compare, because the current memorandum of understanding is an interim arrangement meant to outline a negotiating path to a fuller deal.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament

media:content.media:description: President Trump arrived in Paris on Wednesday and headed a dinner with the French president, Emmanuel Macron, at the Palace of Versailles.

item.title: Florida Appeals Court Strikes Down Ban on Concealed Carry for Young Adults
item.description: The state attorney general, James Uthmeier, had declined to defend the nearly 40-year-old ban on 18- to 20-year-olds carrying concealed weapons. He celebrated the ruling on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: David Ovalle

media:content.media:description: A gun shop in Port Charlotte, Fla.

item.title: Tropical Storm Arthur Forecast: How Much Rain Will Fall?
item.description: Heavy rain and flash flooding are expected across the region through Friday.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones

media:content.media:description: Floodwaters rose in Galveston, Texas, on Wednesday.

item.title: Driver Charged With Hitting ICE Agent After Confusion Over His Identity
item.description: Federal prosecutors said the man had refused to roll down his window when Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers in New Jersey told him to stop.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní and Robin Stein

media:content.media:description: Officials said a federal immigration agent was struck by a vehicle at an intersection in Manahawkin, N.J.

item.title: 18-Year-Old Dies in Fall From Horse Carriage in Central Park
item.description: After the driver stepped out to take a photo of the passengers, the horse bolted, and a tourist from India fell from the driverless carriage. “We were yelling, ‘Help me, help me!’” said his father.
item.dc:creator: Andy Newman, Miles G. Cohen and Chelsia Rose Marcius

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The accident was the latest in a series of mishaps involving horse carriages.

item.title: Seth Rogen on Fame and Insecurity
item.description: Seth Rogen has a very successful career in Hollywood, but that doesn’t mean he hasn’t felt insecure about his talent. He spoke about how his views on money and fame have changed throughout his life on “The Interview.”

item.title: How the U.S.-Iran Deal Came Down to the Wire
item.description: The agreement, which punted many of the toughest issues to future negotiations, came after a last-minute scramble.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Ronen Bergman, Farnaz Fassihi, David E. Sanger, Elian Peltier and Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: President Trump at the U.F.C. fight at the White House on Sunday, on the evening of the deal’s announcement.

item.title: France Assembled an African Dream Team. Senegal Could Not Stop Them.
item.description: Many see the rivalry between France and Senegal as a result of colonial heritage. But at the same time, European teams are more African than ever.
item.dc:creator: Saikou Jammeh

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Eight out of the 11 France players, in blue, who started the match against Senegal on Tuesday, were first- or second-generation immigrants, including Michael Olise, left, 24, born in the U.K. to a Nigerian father and French-Algerian mother.

item.title: Federal Reserve Holds Interest Rates Steady
item.description: In his first news conference as the Federal Reserve chair, Kevin Warsh announced that interest rates would remain unchanged.
item.dc:creator: Alisa Shodiyev Kaff

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kevin M. Warsh, the Federal Reserve’s new chairman, held his first news conference on Wednesday after the central bank voted to keep interest rates unchanged.

item.title: The Race to Save Survivors After a Texas Plane Crash
item.description: One woman’s video captured rescuers rushing to the small jet, which crashed on a Laredo highway. Five people escaped the wreckage, and one died.
item.dc:creator: Edgar Sandoval

media:content.media:description: People trying to pull survivors out of a small plane that crashed on a highway in Laredo, Texas, on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Escalates Tensions With Senate Republicans
item.description: Also, the U.S. shares details of the preliminary Iran deal. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

item.title: Federal Prisons Must Provide Hormones Sought by Trans Inmates
item.description: The government had argued that a “pendulum swing” in the medical consensus justified treating trans inmates with psychotherapy and antidepressants instead.
item.dc:creator: Amy Harmon

media:content.media:description: Judge Royce C. Lamberth of the District of Columbia.
